title: Causal Tyre-Cliff Change-Point Features
summary: Recent F1 tyre papers model degradation as a latent, time-varying process with pit-stop resets, and generic degradation literature shows two-phase change-points are often more informative than raw age.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre papers model degradation as a latent, time-varying process with pit-stop resets, and generic degradation literature shows two-phase change-points are often more informative than raw age. Your current best features encode static service windows but not online evidence that a stint has just entered a failure phase.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year,Race,Driver,Stint)` sequence, fit an online 5-8 lap piecewise trend or Kalman-style filter on `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`; emit causal features such as change-point probability, pre/post slope, residual variance, and laps since detected cliff. Keep computation strictly past-only inside each fold.
expected_effect: Should improve recall on abrupt pit-trigger laps and reduce dependence on hand-set compound life constants.
risk: Noisy laps, traffic, or yellow-flag effects can create false cliffs unless the detector is smoothed or clipped.
